title: Add M4 obligations and town-rule slice
summary: Add focused obligation and executable town-rule owner state for lamp oil duty, lodging witness duty, name confirmation and night-knock compliance.
acceptance: Obligation state owns creditor debtor type condition due window visibility inheritance basis fulfillment action violation consequence source event and current state
TownRule state owns subject scope time region trigger action exception enforcement method cost legitimacy source penalty and state
Lampkeeper oil duty lodging witness duty name confirmation and night-knock compliance are represented as explicit facts with structured reasons
Compliance reads knowledge needs relationship fear enforcement risk emergency and obligation facts through bounded candidates rather than global scans
Focused tests cover fulfillment violation due-index cleanup compliance exceptions enforcement costs structured reasons and no hidden mechanical text

history.details: Created by WM-0061 M4 planning DAG; remains proposed until dependencies are done.

Implemented M4 obligation and town-rule owner stores with explicit numeric facts, structured reasons, bounded due/compliance candidate reads, focused m4-obligations tests, docs, and report; typecheck, focused tests, validate, diff-check, and quality all pass.

changes_requested: High: town-rule compliance ignores stored trigger/action and evaluates every same subject/region/time rule, making those mechanical fields inert; High: stored exception lane is not honored, emergency rejects even rules with exception=0; Medium: obligation due query consumes candidateCap before due-window filtering, so matching later rows can be missed and reported as no_candidate. Required checks and pnpm quality pass mechanically.

Repaired WM-0064 review findings: compliance context now carries trigger/action and confirmed-identity lanes, town rules are indexed by subject/region/trigger/action, stored exception masks are honored, due candidate caps apply after due-window filtering, and focused regressions pass; typecheck, m4-obligations tests, validate, diff-check, and quality all pass.

changes_requested: High: obligation due queries still traverse unbounded non-matching debtor due rows while reporting only matching rows as visited, so metrics/caps understate actor-facing work; Medium: town-rule compliance cap is consumed before time-window filtering, so out-of-time rows in the same subject/region/trigger/action bucket can hide a later in-time rule. Prior trigger/action and exception-mask findings are fixed. Required checks and pnpm quality pass mechanically.

Repaired remaining WM-0064 bounded-read findings: obligation due queries and town-rule compliance now expose explicit scan caps, report scan-cap hits and actual visited counts, and focused regressions cover tail-match/no-match due scans plus out-of-time town-rule rows before in-time matches; typecheck, m4-obligations tests, validate, diff-check, and quality all pass.

verified: No blocking findings. Re-reviewed current worktree including untracked WM-0064 source/tests; obligation due scanCap and town-rule compliance scanCap repairs verified, trigger/action and exception-mask fixes remain intact, and required checks plus pnpm quality pass.
Verified by reviewer after trigger/action, exception-mask, due scanCap and compliance scanCap repairs; required checks and pnpm quality passed.

objective: Implement the minimal obligation and town-rule owner surfaces needed for the M4 borrowed-shadow lamp scenario.
forbiddenPaths: broad governance faction campaign or M5 content framework
public save format or schema changes
hidden narrative flag or text-only rule workaround
benchmarkImpact: Adds due-obligation compliance candidate violation fulfillment and rule-cost counters; baseline updates wait for WM-0070.
reviewRouting: Simulation-engineer completes to reviewer; systems-architect must be consulted before changing obligation ownership, town-rule semantics or save scope.
rollbackModel: Revert obligation/town-rule stores, tests, metrics and docs; preserve prior lamp and Chronicle owner surfaces.
ownerGate: Keep obligations and town rules as owner facts
Do not hide mechanical conditions in text
Use bounded due and compliance indexes
Block on public protocol save schema dependency UI authority broad governance M5 or director scope

sparkEligibility.reason: Obligation and executable town-rule ownership affects deterministic simulation and cannot be assigned to Spark.

review.summary: No blocking findings. Re-reviewed current worktree including untracked WM-0064 source/tests; obligation due scanCap and town-rule compliance scanCap repairs verified, trigger/action and exception-mask fixes remain intact, and required checks plus pnpm quality pass.

integration.summary: Verified by reviewer after trigger/action, exception-mask, due scanCap and compliance scanCap repairs; required checks and pnpm quality passed.
